prerequisites: Your Flexmail account ID, found in your Flexmail account settings (used as the account_id config field).
A personal access token created in Flexmail under Settings > API > Personal access tokens (used as the personal_access_token config field).

authMethods.name: Personal access token (HTTP Basic)
authMethods.summary: Flexmail authenticates API requests using HTTP Basic authentication. Your account ID is the username and a personal access token is the password. Provide both as the account_id and personal_access_token fields.

setupSteps.title: Find your Flexmail account ID
setupSteps.body: Sign in to Flexmail and locate your account ID in your account settings. Enter this value in the account_id field. It is used as the username for HTTP Basic authentication.

setupSteps.title: Create a personal access token
setupSteps.body: In the Flexmail dashboard, go to Settings > API > Personal access tokens and create a new token with a descriptive name. Copy the token immediately, since it is shown only once. Enter it in the personal_access_token field. It is used as the password for HTTP Basic authentication.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Provide both the account_id and personal_access_token. The connector uses them together as HTTP Basic credentials to authenticate against the Flexmail API.

sources.title: Getting started with the transactional API - Flexmail Manual
